summary: Re-linked the Dolphin memory card runtime library objects by changing six `dolphin/card` source files from `NonMatching` to `Matching` in `configure.py`. The source edits are small matching/linkage fixes: always emitting previously `MUST_MATCH`-guarded dummy stack arrays, adding `#pragma force_active` around externally-needed or otherwise retained functions, exporting `CreateCallbackFat`, and changing one conditional from `MUST_MATCH` to `!BUGFIX` for a suspected missing-inline/bugfix-sensitive assignment.
what_changed: `configure.py` now marks `dolphin/card/CARDUnlock.c`, `CARDRdwr.c`, `CARDBlock.c`, `CARDDir.c`, `CARDCreate.c`, and `CARDStat.c` as `Object(Matching, ...)` instead of `Object(NonMatching, ...)` in the `RuntimeLib` card object list.

Previously `#ifdef MUST_MATCH`-guarded unused stack buffers `u8 _[8];` were made unconditional in `CARDBlock.c`, `CARDDir.c`, and `CARDUnlock.c`, preserving the stack-layout effect without relying on `MUST_MATCH`.
`__CARDAllocBlock` in `CARDBlock.c` was wrapped in `#pragma push`, `#pragma force_active on`, and `#pragma pop` so the symbol remains emitted/active.

A duplicated-looking `card = &__CARDBlock[chan];` pattern in `__CARDFreeBlock` changed its guard from `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` to `#ifndef BUGFIX`, documenting that the odd code is tied to non-bugfixed matching behavior rather than only a match-only build mode.
`CreateCallbackFat` in `CARDCreate.c` was changed from `static void` to external `void`, guarded with `#pragma force_active` when `BUGFIX` is not defined, and declared in `CARDCreate.h`.
`CARDCreateAsync`, `CARDGetXferredBytes`, and `CARDSetStatusAsync` were wrapped with `#pragma force_active on`, with comments noting they are used only by `#hsd_803AAA48`.
No PR body, issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the supplied context; evidence is from the title, changed-file list, and diff excerpt.
smart_moves: The PR separated true matching/linkage mechanics from old `MUST_MATCH` scaffolding by making necessary dummy stack reservations unconditional, which lets the files be promoted to `Matching` cleanly.
Using localized `#pragma push`/`#pragma force_active on`/`#pragma pop` blocks retained required symbols without globally affecting compiler behavior.
The `#ifndef BUGFIX` guard communicates that a suspicious assignment pattern is intentional for original-code matching and should be omitted only in bugfixed builds.
Adding `CreateCallbackFat` to `CARDCreate.h` matches its new non-static linkage and avoids relying on an internal-only declaration after the symbol is force-retained/exported.
The comments `Used only by #hsd_803AAA48` preserve why otherwise-unused public CARD functions need force-active treatment, which is useful for future dead-code/link-order investigations.
decomp_lessons: When moving runtime-library objects from `NonMatching` to `Matching`, check for local `MUST_MATCH` hacks that can be converted into normal source constructs once they are known to be required for the retail binary.
Unused local arrays can be legitimate stack-shape matching tools; if they are required for the target binary, keep them in source rather than hiding them behind `MUST_MATCH`.
If a function is only referenced through obscure external code or linker behavior, `#pragma force_active` can be a targeted way to preserve it while keeping the rest of the file normal.
Functions made non-static for linkage or force-active reasons should have header declarations updated at the same time.
Use semantic build guards like `BUGFIX` when a strange construct represents an original-code bug or artifact, rather than treating it as merely a decompilation matching hack.
A relink PR can consist largely of configuration flips plus tiny source-layout/linkage corrections; those small edits are often what make a whole library eligible for `Matching`.
naming_conventions: Dolphin CARD source files retain SDK-style names such as `CARDBlock.c`, `CARDCreate.c`, `CARDDir.c`, `CARDRdwr.c`, `CARDStat.c`, and `CARDUnlock.c`.

Internal CARD helpers use double-underscore names such as `__CARDAllocBlock`, `__CARDFreeBlock`, `__CARDUpdateFatBlock`, `__CARDUpdateDir`, `__CARDWrite`, `__CARDWritePage`, `__CARDPutControlBlock`, and `__CARDUnlock`.
Public CARD APIs use SDK-style names such as `CARDCreateAsync`, `CARDGetXferredBytes`, `CARDGetStatus`, and `CARDSetStatusAsync`.
Callback symbols use descriptive CamelCase names including `EraseCallback`, `WriteCallback`, `BlockWriteCallback`, and `CreateCallbackFat`.
The dummy unused stack buffer is named `_`, as in `u8 _[8];`, indicating intentional unused storage for layout matching.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Promoted six CARD objects to `Matching` in the runtime library object list.
Kept unused `u8 _[8];` stack allocations unconditionally to preserve stack frame shape.
Applied `#pragma force_active on` only around specific symbols that must be retained: `__CARDAllocBlock`, `CreateCallbackFat`, `CARDCreateAsync`, `CARDGetXferredBytes`, and `CARDSetStatusAsync`.
Used `#pragma push` and `#pragma pop` to scope force-active pragmas tightly.

Changed a match-only conditional from `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` to `#ifndef BUGFIX` around an odd assignment in `__CARDFreeBlock`, implying the retail behavior is intentionally preserved except in bugfix builds.
Converted `CreateCallbackFat` from `static` to external linkage and added a header prototype, likely to satisfy symbol/link expectations during relink.
follow_up_queries: Find other PRs that converted `MUST_MATCH` unused stack arrays into unconditional dummy buffers.
Search for other `#pragma force_active` uses around functions referenced only by `hsd_803AAA48`.
Compare later CARD PRs to see whether the `#ifndef BUGFIX` assignment in `__CARDFreeBlock` was refined or documented further.
Look for relink PRs that promote several `Object(NonMatching, ...)` entries to `Object(Matching, ...)` after small linkage fixes.
